revision.comment: Bot: Create shrine page for Kawazoe Shrine
Copying from [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] to [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Text replacement - "{{wikidata link|Q1}}" to ""

Text replacement - "{{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}}" to "{{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]]"
Text replacement - "is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō." to "is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]."
Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
Text replacement - "RONSHA" to "{{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|WD=Q135022904}}[[Category:RONSHA]]"
Text replacement - "Category:RONSHA" to "Category:Shikinai Ronsha"
Bot: Generate standardized Shikinaisha page (upgraded script - full property sections, ILL links, P361 categorization)
Bot: Generate standardized Shikinaisha page (V8 - proper ILL deity links, P13677 URLs, province extraction)

{{nihongo|'''Kawazoe Shrine'''|川添神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawazoe Shrine'''|川添神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawazoe Shrine'''|川添神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawazoe Shrine'''|川添神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawazoe Shrine'''|川添神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawazoe Shrine'''|川添神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2025年12月}}
{{神社
| 名称 = 川添神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=多気郡}}
| 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|大台町|Q1204505}}栃原1481
| 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| 祭神 = 建日別命・{{jalink|天手力雄命}}・{{jalink|天照大御神}}
| 神体 = 
| 社格 =  {{jalink|式内社}}論社、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
| 創建 = 
| 本殿 = 
| 別名 = 
| 札所等 = 
| 例祭 = 4月15日
| 神事 =

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''川添神社'''（かわぞえじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|大台町|Q1204505}}栃原にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}多気郡の{{jalink|式内社}}である相鹿牟山神社の論社で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
{{jalink|大和朝廷}}成立期、伊勢・熊野周辺の国造であった建日別命は、栃原周辺を統治した。そこへ産土神として{{jalink|天手力雄命}}とともに、江戸時代以前に祀られ「建日別神社」と呼ばれるようになったことが創祀と考えられている。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年、{{jalink|川添村 (三重県)|Q17176088|川添村}}の村社7社無格社4社境内社59社を合祀して、「川添神社」と改称する<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 川添神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=2025-12-18}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 川添神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
建日別命・{{jalink|天手力雄命}}・{{jalink|天照大御神}}・{{jalink|天忍穂耳命}}・{{jalink|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天津彦根命}}・{{jalink|活津彦根命}}・その他全三十座<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== 境内 ===
*祖霊社
*{{要出典範囲|神宮遙拝所|date=2025年12月}}
=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：4月3日
*春分祭：春分の日
*秋分・慰霊祭：秋分の日
*新穀感謝祭：11月23日
*厄除・祝賀報告祭：2月11日
*五身懸祭：2月19日直近の日曜日（[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/64 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載されている）

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== 関連項目 ===

'''川添神社'''（かわぞえじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|大台町|Q1204505}}栃原にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}多気郡の{{jalink|式内社}}である相鹿牟山神社の論社で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
{{jalink|大和朝廷}}成立期、伊勢・熊野周辺の国造であった建日別命は、栃原周辺を統治した。そこへ産土神として{{jalink|天手力雄命}}とともに、江戸時代以前に祀られ「建日別神社」と呼ばれるようになったことが創祀と考えられている。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年、{{jalink|川添村 (三重県)|Q17176088|川添村}}の村社7社無格社4社境内社59社を合祀して、「川添神社」と改称する<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 川添神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=2025-12-18}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 川添神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
建日別命・{{jalink|天手力雄命}}・{{jalink|天照大御神}}・{{jalink|天忍穂耳命}}・{{jalink|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天津彦根命}}・{{jalink|活津彦根命}}・その他全三十座<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== 境内 ===
*祖霊社
*{{要出典範囲|神宮遙拝所|date=2025年12月}}
=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：4月3日
*春分祭：春分の日
*秋分・慰霊祭：秋分の日
*新穀感謝祭：11月23日
*厄除・祝賀報告祭：2月11日
*五身懸祭：2月19日直近の日曜日（[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/64 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載されている）

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== 関連項目 ===

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2025年12月}}
{{神社
| 名称 = 川添神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=多気郡}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''川添神社'''（かわぞえじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|多気郡|Q1042360}}{{jalink|大台町|Q1204505}}栃原にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}多気郡の{{jalink|式内社}}である相鹿牟山神社の論社で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 由緒 ===
{{jalink|大和朝廷}}成立期、伊勢・熊野周辺の国造であった建日別命は、栃原周辺を統治した。そこへ産土神として{{jalink|天手力雄命}}とともに、江戸時代以前に祀られ「建日別神社」と呼ばれるようになったことが創祀と考えられている。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年、{{jalink|川添村 (三重県)|Q17176088|川添村}}の村社7社無格社4社境内社59社を合祀して、「川添神社」と改称する<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 川添神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=2025-12-18}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 川添神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
建日別命・{{jalink|天手力雄命}}・{{jalink|天照大御神}}・{{jalink|天忍穂耳命}}・{{jalink|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天津彦根命}}・{{jalink|活津彦根命}}・その他全三十座<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== 境内 ===
*祖霊社
*{{要出典範囲|神宮遙拝所|date=2025年12月}}
=== 祭儀 ===
*例祭：4月3日
*春分祭：春分の日
*秋分・慰霊祭：秋分の日
*新穀感謝祭：11月23日
*厄除・祝賀報告祭：2月11日
*五身懸祭：2月19日直近の日曜日（[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/64 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載されている）

=== 脚注 ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== 関連項目 ===
* {{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}
* {{jalink|三重県の神社一覧|Q48761540}}

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Tochihara, {{jalink|Ōdai Town|Q1204505}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Taki District in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{jalink|Yamato court}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{jalink|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{jalink|Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|Kawazoe Village}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{jalink|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} · {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

=== Notes ===
{{Help:Footnotes}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}}
* {{jalink|List of Shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawazoe Shrine}}

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Tochihara, {{jalink|Ōdai Town|Q1204505}}, {{jalink|Taki District|Q1042360}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Taki District in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{jalink|Yamato court}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{jalink|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{jalink|Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|Kawazoe Village}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{jalink|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} · {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}} · {{jalink|Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

=== Notes ===
{{Help:Footnotes}}
{{Reflist|2}}

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}}
* {{jalink|List of Shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawazoe Shrine}}

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|WD=Q1204505}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill||lt=Yamato court|UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|WD=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} · {{ill||lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|UNKNOWN}} · {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} · {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} · {{ill||lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} · {{ill||lt=Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|WD=Q1204505}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|WD=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|WD=Q1204505}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|WD=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q455602}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|WD=Q1204505|1=Ōdai}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|WD=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village|1=Kawazoe}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|WD=Q1204505|1=Ōdai}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|WD=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village|1=Kawazoe}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|WD=Q1204505|1=Ōdai}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|WD=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village|1=Kawazoe}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
Takehiwake-no-Mikoto · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} · and others, totaling thirty deities<ref name="県神社庁"/>

=== Shrine grounds ===
* Ancestral Spirit Shrine
* {{Citation needed span|Jingū Remote Worship Hall|date=December 2025}}

=== Rituals ===
* Annual festival: April 3
* Vernal Equinox Festival: Vernal Equinox Day
* Autumnal Equinox & Memorial Festival: Autumnal Equinox Day
* New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival: November 23
* Yakuyoke (misfortune prevention) & Celebration Report Festival: February 11
* Goshinkake Festival: The Sunday closest to February 19 (listed in “Special Shinto Rituals in Mie Prefecture”)

'''Kawazoe Shrine''' (川添神社, Kawazoe-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tochihara, {{ill|lt=Ōdai Town|Q1204505|qid=Q1204505|1=Ōdai}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|qid=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a disputed shrine (ronsha) of Aikamu-yama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} of Taki District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== History ===
During the formative period of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takehiwake-no-Mikoto, who served as a kuni-no-miyatsuko (provincial chieftain) of the Ise and Kumano regions, governed the area around Tochihara. Together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he came to be enshrined as the local tutelary deity sometime before the Edo period, and the shrine became known as “Takehiwake Shrine,” which is considered its founding origin. In the 40th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1907), seven village shrines, four unranked shrines, and 59 subordinate shrines within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kawazoe Village (Mie Prefecture)|Q17176088|qid=Q17176088|lt=Kawazoe Village|1=Kawazoe}} were merged and the shrine was renamed “Kawazoe Shrine.”<ref name="県神社庁">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 18, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Kawazoe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2885|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>
